headline: MRT3 Circle Line Capped at RM45 Billion — Down from RM68 Billion in 2018
context: MRT3 received final approval in 2025 with a budget cap of approximately RM45 billion — about RM31 billion for construction and RM6-7 billion for land acquisition. The 2018 estimate had been RM68 billion before cancellation and revival.

cards.big: MRT3 Circle Line capped at RM45 billion — reduced from the RM68 billion 2018 estimate before cancellation and revival.
cards.sub: Construction allocation about RM31 billion plus RM6-7 billion for land acquisition. Final approval received in 2025.

cards.big: The project was cancelled in 2018 over cost concerns and revived in 2023 — every revival round adds review layers without binding caps.
cards.sub: Land acquisition is targeted for completion in 2026; construction expected from 2027.

cards.big: 51.6km elevated circle with 10 interchange stations linking existing MRT, LRT, KTM and Monorail lines.
cards.sub: Designed to close the Klang Valley rail loop. Operational target: 2032.

cards.big: Even at the reduced cap, MRT3 remains Malaysia's largest single rail project — fiscal scrutiny continues at every revision.
cards.sub: Independent audit of engineering and procurement cost assumptions has been called for at multiple cost-revision rounds.

cards.big: The story is not 'budget ballooning' — it is whether even the reduced cap holds through construction.
Reducing the headline figure does not eliminate cost-overrun risk. The discipline test is whether 2027-2032 construction stays within RM45 billion.
